content.full_text:
Administration 
Enforcement 
•• Statements of Economic Interest 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322·5662 
322·5660 
Executive/Legal 
322·5901 
322-6441 
322-6444 
David M. Kennedy 
Office of the City Attorney 
200 Lincoln Avenue 
Salinas, CA 
93901 
Dear Mr. Kennedy: 
February 5, 1985 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our Advice No. A-85-013 
Thank you for your letter requesting information on behalf 
of Salinas City Councilmember Russ Jeffries. 
In your letter, 
you concluded that an Individual Retirement Account (IRA), 
solely invested in a savings account with Great Western Savings, 
is exempt from disclosure and requested our concurrence. 
An IRA, worth more than $1,000, must be reported unless the 
IRA is totally invested in a mutual fund registered with the SEC 
or some other type of investment exempt from disclosure (e.g., 
time or demand deposits in savings accounts, treasury bills, 
government bonds). 
In addition, any investment or interest in real property 
held by an IRA must be reported unless: 
the funds are pooled with other investors and your 
interest in the IRA, or any particular interest held by 
the IRA, is less than 10 percent; or 
the investments are of the type exempt from disclosure 
(e.g., treasury bills, government bonds). 
Your conclusion is correct. 
The IRA held by Councilmember 
Jeffries, invested solely in a savings account, is exempt from 
disclosure. 


David M. Kennedy 
February 5, 1985 
Page 2 
Should you have further questions on this matter, please do 
not hesitate to contact me. 
JET:plh 
Sincerely, 
Jeanette E. Turvill 
Legal Assistant 
Legal Division 


January 23, 1985 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Re: 
Councilman Russ Jeffries 
Councilman Russ Jeffries has an interest in an IRA with Great 
Western Savings. 
The IRA is invested solely in a savings 
account. 
I have taken the position that the IRA is not reportable as it 
is an investment in a savings account and therefore exempt from 
disclosure. 
Please be so kind as to review my conclusion. 
Very truly yours, 
DAVID M. KENNEDY 
City Attorney 
DMK:jb 


P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
has been referred to Jeanette Turvill of our Legal Division. 
If 
you have any questions about your advice request, you may 
contact Ms. Turvill directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions 
or unless more information is needed to answer your request, you 
should expect a response within 21 working days. 
Very truly yours, 
AaJU)tJ UL:f~r' »dbIU/~

sections.question: Councilman Russ Jeffries has an interest in an IRA with Great Western Savings. The IRA is invested solely in a savings account. I have taken the position that the IRA is not reportable as it is an investment in a savings account and therefore exempt from disclosure. Please be so kind as to review my conclusion.
sections.conclusion: An IRA, worth more than $1,000, must be reported unless the IRA is totally invested in a mutual fund registered with the SEC or some other type of investment exempt from disclosure (e.g., time or demand deposits in savings accounts, treasury bills, government bonds). In addition, any investment or interest in real property held by an IRA must be reported unless: the funds are pooled with other investors and your interest in the IRA, or any particular interest held by the IRA, is less than 10 percent; or the investments are of the type exempt from disclosure (e.g., treasury bills, government bonds). Your conclusion is correct. The IRA held by Councilmember Jeffries, invested solely in a savings account, is exempt from disclosure.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether an Individual Retirement Account (IRA) invested solely in a savings account held by a city councilmember must be reported under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concurs that the IRA invested solely in a savings account is exempt from disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act because savings account deposits are an exempt type of investment.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (dated January 23, 1985) and the FPPC's formal response (dated February 5, 1985). The response letter confirms the requestor's conclusion regarding IRA disclosure exemptions.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Councilman Russ Jeffries has an interest in an IRA with Great Western Savings. The IRA is invested solely in a savings account. I have taken the position that the IRA is not reportable as it is an investment in a savings account and therefore exempt from disclosure. Please be so kind as to review my conclusion.

CONCLUSION: An IRA, worth more than $1,000, must be reported unless the IRA is totally invested in a mutual fund registered with the SEC or some other type of investment exempt from disclosure (e.g., time or demand deposits in savings accounts, treasury bills, government bonds). In addition, any investment or interest in real property held by an IRA must be reported unless: the funds are pooled with other investors and your interest in the IRA, or any particular interest held by the IRA, is less than 10 percent; or the investments are of the type exempt from disclosure (e.g., treasury bills, government bonds). Your conclusion is correct. The IRA held by Councilmember Jeffries, invested solely in a savings account, is exempt from disclosure.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to an inquiry from the Salinas City Attorney regarding whether Councilmember Russ Jeffries' IRA invested solely in a savings account with Great Western Savings must be disclosed. The FPPC confirms that such an IRA is exempt from disclosure.
